cards.title: How Children Boost Parental Brain Health
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics from parenting may be the secret to cognitive benefits.

cards.title: Creativity Flourishes When You Become a Parent
cards.body: One parent discovered that having kids didn't kill creativity—it transformed it entirely. Everyday moments, like a child turning kale into imaginary giraffes, spark unexpected inspiration.

cards.title: Managing Teenage Alcohol Issues on Family Holidays
cards.body: A family's holiday was derailed when their teenage daughter arrived drunk before a 12-hour drive. The incident created tension that lasted beyond the trip itself.

cards.title: Summer Self-Care for Parents in Recovery
cards.body: Summer's disrupted routines and increased childcare demands can challenge parents managing recovery. Maintaining self-care practices during seasonal changes is essential for parental wellbeing.

cards.title: Games in Therapy Work for Children and Teens
cards.body: Using games in therapy sessions with children and teens is highly effective for engagement and intervention. This therapeutic approach is sometimes underestimated but proves its value repeatedly.

cards.title: Good Parenting Builds Leadership and Life Skills
cards.body: Strong parenting practices directly develop leadership abilities and essential life skills in children. The lessons parents teach shape how children navigate challenges throughout their lives.
